item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reading the discussion makes me wanna look at the older versions for comparison.

Also I'm a pug owner so it piqued my interest immediately.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as it comes from here with no indication it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting but kind of dumby dumb dumb, the SH tie-in is cool, but makes them look like morons and the scip becomes too much joke-like (Everyone running around, comitting suicide, making terrible compromises and whatnot because of a not so particularly dangerous dog). As it stands now, you would do better playing it as a cardboard dog that makes you overestimate danger, hyperbolise and become infuriatingly melodramatic. If you could make it so subtly without stating it outright in the scip then it would be genius… actually, who is to say that wasn't your original intent? Oh my God, it works! That's my new headcanon about 1783. Upvoted

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Since a big draw on this article is how desperate the SH is to get rid of cardboard dog, it might behoove the reader to get to see the kind of dangerous shit the SH interact with on a regular day for comparison. The most efficient way of doing that would be to create a link in here to the SH hub. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

SCP-1783 was brought to Foundation attention during an apparent attack against Foundation Site 38 on 11/02/1999. Four individuals, identified as having some association with Group of Interest Sigma-5 ("Serpent's Hand"), penetrated the perimeter of Site 38, entered the building in force carrying a small container, surrendered the container to Foundation security forces, and promptly committed suicide via cyanide ingestion. The container held SCP-1783 and a note, purportedly written by [DATA REDACTED—LEVEL 4 CLEARANCE REQUIRED].

To this:

SCP-1783 was brought to Foundation attention during an apparent attack against Foundation Site 38 on 11/02/1999. Four individuals, identified as having some association with Group of Interest Sigma-5 ("Serpent's Hand"), penetrated the perimeter of Site 38, entered the building in force carrying a small container, surrendered the container to Foundation security forces, and promptly committed suicide via cyanide ingestion. The container held SCP-1783 and a note, purportedly written by [DATA REDACTED—LEVEL 4 CLEARANCE REQUIRED].

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the idea of this in general, but it was mostly a neutral article for me until hitting the Hand tie-in. That was very well executed, and swings it from uncertain to a positive +1. Good little cardboard dog.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i'm with dark on this someone make a papercraft of this guy, but i also agree that i'm a no vote.. still things that bug me but not enough to make me hate it :B

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't like or dislike enough to vote, because: The concept isn't great overall. It shits pictures. Whoopdy doo. That said, I really enjoy the Serpent's Hand addition. While I don't think you pulled off the actual article, I think you pulled of the GoI tie in. In my headcanon the Serpent's Hand is just disorganized and underequipped enough to be seriously terrified of a relatively harmless SCP like this one. Imagine you keep a relatively harmless pug around for the lulz and then, suddenly, it freaks the hell out and starts killing people. And it won't LEAVE YOU ALONE. I for one can definitely imagine them panicking and dumping it at the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In this document Serpent's Hand is listed as GOI Sigma-5, whereas this document lists Serpent's Hand as Alpha-019. Can someone please eliminate the discrepancy?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
were observed to peter out

This just doesn't feel very clinical to me, maybe change it to "were observed to end" or something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love how it scares the non-primate animals, what could they sense. Another great idea is the "trying to make a master, but something went wrong." All around a nice SCP. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These are edits I considered making, but I was worried they wouldn't seem realistic. The original death count was about six or so. I'll make edits later today.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Hand wouldn't like destroying an SCP. However, there are certainly members who'd be willing to do so. They'd consider it self-defense. Other members would doubtless grow angry with them, and there would be debates, accusations, name-calling, and so forth before some other crisis came up.

I don't see this thing having managed to kill twenty-four of them before they shut it in a crate. Even if they didn't decide to destroy it, they're not going to just let it run free while it's attacking them. I'd lower the deathcount a bit. Make it a half dozen or so. That's still a lot of them, considering they're a small organization.

I'd also limit the number of Serpent's Hand "units" who entered. For one thing, when you're discussing "units," you're usually discussing groups of people. So when you say "multiple units," it sounds like they had a bunch of squads running into the site. Like, four or five dozen people, at least. I'd have it down to two or three people. Enough to have one carry the box and one or two more to keep watch and help get it past fences and the like. I just don't see the Hand risking more than that. Even if they're already going to die from the bacterium, they don't want to risk them not being able to commit suicide when needed, allowing the Foundation to question them and learn too much about the Hand. Keep in mind, the Hand aren't commandos. If two people can't make it through the Foundation perimeter with just their know-how, several dozen aren't going to be able to either.

It's apparently better than it was originally, but it's still written in a way that only works if the Hand is composed of idiots.

item.title: THE SENSE-MAKENING: ROUNDS 1 AND 2
item.content:encoded:
Okay, as Moose mentioned above, I made some edits to make the Serpent's Hand thing make more sense, and clarified the containment procedures to make them not stupid. Voct edited them into a more sensible order. Hope you enjoy.

EDIT: Voct literally just explained what people meant by "y u sacrificing personnel" in the comments. That was a weird half-formed idea I had left in and never thought to change. His edit did away with that too, so let's all give him a clap.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
:D

I wrote and kept in the note at the end out of a fairly genuine sense that the Serpent's Hand is one of the genuinely "good" GoIs, occasionally thinking of the betterment of others, or at least protecting them from death.

item.title: Re: Suggestion
item.content:encoded:
The Hand operatives who went over were… anomalous, in and of themselves. They would have died within 48 hours anyway. They were insta-clones. They're the Hand's equivalent to D-class. Because they died by cyanide capsule, their corpses won't disintegrate into glitter (or… whatever), and will just look like regular corpses.

The Serpent's Hand is a lot more willing to use and exploit and cross-test anomalous shit than the Foundation is.

Voct, this is really cool. It's not what Eskobar ended up editing into this article, but it's still neat. If I do a Serpent's Hand piece, I'll possibly incorporate something like that (depending on what splinter group I go with).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This edit is much better, despite only minor changes being made.

I like that you added the encryption aspect in particular. And I thought you'd have to cut "Keep everyone safe. Please." to make me like the note, but it turns out that your edits to the opening lines counterbalance it just fine.

Maybe there are still issues, but I don't see them at the moment and all the bothersome stuff has been sufficiently ameliorated. Now I can finally upvote this thing. +2 swing

item.title: Re: Suggestion
item.content:encoded:
The Hand operatives who went over were… anomalous, in and of themselves. They would have died within 48 hours anyway. They were insta-clones. They're the Hand's equivalent to D-class. Because they died by cyanide capsule, their corpses won't disintegrate into glitter (or… whatever), and will just look like regular corpses.

The Serpent's Hand is a lot more willing to use and exploit and cross-test anomalous shit than the Foundation is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If my responses have been rather passionate it's because I really like the core idea of this SCP but keep getting tripped up by certain aspects of the execution.

Seconding this. :)

item.title: Re: Suggestion
item.content:encoded: I like this suggestion, Voct, but it makes the Hand stuff come off even stupider than it already does. Which is really quite stupid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're welcome. If my responses have been rather passionate it's because I really like the core idea of this SCP but keep getting tripped up by certain aspects of the execution.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks everyone for the helpful (in this case, helpfully specific) suggestions. I am reading them, and will make edits for internal consistency at first opportunity. I work a lot at the beginning of the week, but I should be able to get some edits in later in the week.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well then I would appreciate it if that were in the article. Right now it just says it's made of ordinary cardboard and I have trouble taking it seriously…

item.title: Re: Suggestion
item.content:encoded:
I like this. It's more sensible. As it stands the unending rage state is too much like SCP-096 without the ability to tear through walls to get at its target, and with less justification.

As written by Voct it would still be a big hassle and necessitate blood screening for site personnel.

As for the Serpent's Hand stuff, I like the idea of one GOI ditching a SCP on the Foundation that they're unable to handle, but I don't think this is the right SCP for that. There are two solid ideas in this article but they're jammed together unnecessarily.

item.title: Suggestion
item.content:encoded:
Let's moderate the response a bit.

It still goes into a frothing rage whenever it detects B antigens, and it can still convert whoever it bites into cardboard…

… but if we don't give it the B-antigen bearer, and we keep B-antigen people away from it, it calms down after a few days. Not a few hours, a few days — during which it might well bite anyone who gets too close.

We don't have to sacrifice people to it when it goes into a rage. We can just let it tantrum itself into exhaustion. But that's not very good for its health, so we should avoid provoking it.

As for the Serpent's Hand suicides… meh, still thinking about it. I have some ideas but they're a bit too vague.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If the dog is as dangerous as it's made out to be, then maybe all of them were bitten. Why the report wouldn't mention it? Dunno, maybe the Foundation just didn't care to mention it or their autopsies are in a different file, something like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What if the Hand members were infected? Then that would justify the cyanide pills.

Yes, I even had that in the initial draft of that post.

But just how many of these guys are infected? All of them? Why wouldn't the report mention that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if the Hand members were infected? Then that would justify the cyanide pills.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just thought of a way this thing might be difficult to contain: what if it's "teleport to imitate stop motion" makes it hard to carry or restrain, since it is actually changing location every time it moves without passing through normal space (or something like that)?

That would fix pretty much all the problems I have with this article, except for the cyanide pill thing which certain people ( >:[ ) have talked me out of hating so much - i.e. maybe the Hand people only took the pill as a last ditch thing once they figured out they weren't going to escape, and while the article itself doesn't indicate that, who says the Foundation are portraying it accurately? It's in their interest to caricature the Hand as a bunch of crazies.

Man that is a lot of fan wank, oh well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just thought of a way this thing might be difficult to contain: what if it's "teleport to imitate stop motion" makes it hard to carry or restrain, since it is actually changing location every time it moves without passing through normal space (or something like that)?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Keep in mind that (at least in my headcanon) the Serpent's Hand is far less equipped than the Foundation to contain any SCP that isn't consciously cooperating with them.

It just needs a kennel that you never let it out of.

I could go buy one right now and I am not exactly rolling in wealth and super-equipment here.

But I guess now I'm just repeating what I (and others) have already said.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That level of desperate. That's what this thing was to the Serpent's Hand.

But it's just a dog!

I mean, can it teleport through walls? If so, how did the Hand operative even keep it in that goddamn container!? And if it can, why is it Safe and not Euclid.

Seriously you guys it's just a dog.

Also any way you cut it, the suicide thing is bullshit, especially if the Hand is concerned about losing more members. Which they're seriously NOT concerned about, since there was no one forcing them to feed people to the dog. They just thought that would be a cool idea I guess. Which is extremely contrived.

And so are the containment procedures, now that I think about it, since the Foundation is feeding 'just anyone' to the skip for no reason except to stop its rage stage. Why do we even care about stopping its rage state. This thing is Safe! What is it going to do, bark and gnaw at the mental container it's in? It's not like it can change the container to cardboard with its bite (which might actually be interesting).

edit: or teleport out, for that matter, which some people assumed was implied already by the article (but isn't actually in there).

(btw, a nitpick, shouldn't the cardboard bite thing just be in the Description, with a better explanation?)

Man this gets more hinky the more I think about it. :( Oh well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My interpretation of relations between the groups matches Eskobar's. This angle makes a good idea a fantastic one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is a teleporter. Who knows how it reacts to fire?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My big question here is why wasn't the Chaos Insurgency able to destroy it? Isn't it just cardboard? Couldn't they just burn it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Had to dowvote for the overblown containment.
It's just not dangeroud enough to 1)bar 1/5 of all potential staff from an entire facility 2)keep a dedicated vet on staff to monitor it's "poop" multiple times per day 3)sacrifice a human resource too if it ever gets mad.
It's a good idea, but it's just really not dangerous, and having everyone from SH to Foundation O5s behaving as if it's the scond coming of 173 just doesn't work.

Of course, that's just my opinion. Apparantly 3 dozen other people don't see it that way.

Edit in 2019: A relatively minor rewrite happened and all my objections above are now void. Switched to +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"When SCP-1783-A is in good health" - I think you intended this to say "When SCP-1783 is in good health". When the pug is in good health, the instances will depict a bowel yadda yadda.

It's too bad you can't photograph farting. That would really capture the pug angle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because perhaps the site director is a dog person. It is still properly contained, the O5 have no fears of it running loose, biting personally, or both. We know how to stop it, and things are more cooperative when happy. Even dogs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It doesn't even need to be a separate group. The Hand can have factions.

True that, I put a splinter group in SCP-1000 myself.

But the behavior is still so odd that it really needs to be specially justified in the SCP itself, from my POV. I would say this for any GOI. Handwaving it in my head isn't enough.

Also, pauli, I laughed. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Tunnel Snakes rule!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…

It could happen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Yeah, every so often someone gets the Serpent's Thumb up his ass, and goes off to form his own faction. They never last, though…"

meanwhile

"That's right, we're the Serpent's combination crack suicide squad and pizza delivery service. What of it?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It doesn't even need to be a seperate group. The Hand can have factions.

Doesn't the Library also demonstrate how disorganised they can be?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: well, at some point, that safe might rupture from being stuffed with too many polaroids of unhealthy dogshit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it overall. I think it's neat.

But.

The Serpent's Hand note feels so nonsensical to me that I have to downvote. Maybe it's just that I'm influenced by Library-related stuff, but I just can't see any SH member writing that, ever. Agreeing with Ink_Asylum that they'd have just quarantined it - it's just a friggin' dog! - and/or sent it to another dimension, depending on your headcanon re: what the Hand has access to. And the suicide thing is just silly in context of the Hand. And the 'please'? The Hand saying Please to the Foundation? Whaaat? I know you don't like Incident Report X23, but that's a central piece establishing the Hand's attitude.

Maybe in your headcanon the Hand actively worship SCPs and therefore see human life as worth less than SCPs (but not worthless, so they'd feed the SCP and sacrifice themselves for it but try to pawn it off on the Foundation at the certain point) but that's so drastically different from all other portrayals of the Hand (feel free to correct me if I'm wrong!) that it requires special justification.

If you change it to another, made-up 'we worship SCPs' group with the traits listed in the previous paragraph, I'd upvote. But for now, -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I imagined that the serpent's hand didn't aggree with the foundation's policy of locking away good people, but it was more like the feelings between the Foundation and the GOC. As in, not hatred but more like you're doing it wrong. And that the Serpent's Hand approved of the foundation containing evil sapient beings, and everything else not sapient.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally, I would have killed it with fire the first time it turned somebody into a cardboard cut-out… Or just shoved it into a safe and made sure it didn't get out. What's stopping anyone in-universe from doing that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And when 1783 goes rabid, it's gonna bite whatever it sees until you finally give it what it wants.

3. Things what we should do what they say becuz we wouldn't like them when they're angry.

I think that's one of the reasons I really dislike the overblown danger in this article. There are two organizations willing to sacrifice valuable and innocent employees to something that is no more powerful than a regular dog. Not even a big dog, a pug!

If you were running a no-kill dog shelter and someone brought in a rabid pug that would fly off the handle every time it smelled 1 out of 5 people, and it wouldn't calm down until you let it maul that exact person to death, who in their right mind would consider allowing that to happen?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, I slept on it, and I'm going to have to downvote for the following reasons:

  1. There's no justification for sacrificing unfortunate personnel to this thing. If it goes into a "rage state" just for sniffing out B-antigens why can't they just leave it in a rage state? You've gone out of your way to point out how it's very similar in behavior to a real dog, so why can't they just treat it like a rabid dog from that point on and keep it locked away? How is returning it to a calm state worth murdering a valuable employee?
  2. The Serpent's Hand section is just too ridiculous to be believable. Again, this thing is no more powerful than a normal dog beyond having a deadly bite. Yet they let 24 people get bitten by it, then sacrificed more delivering it to the Foundation. I don't get it.

For groups that regularly deal with SCPs this is a fairly easy one to deal with, yet your containment procedures and recovery incident gratuitously overinflate the threat and thus the response of the groups involved, for what looks like no other reason than to make the article unnecessarily dramatic. I like the core idea, I really do, but those two points just bother me too much. Fix them and you'll gladly have my upvote and, in my opinion, a much better article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really? Huh. I've never known the Serpent's Hand to really hate the Foundation as much as they are just largely indifferent. But then I suppose that can be rationalised as factional differences.

Oh, and I might add, the SH connection was a large part of why I upvoted. In addtion to the fact that this is just adorable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mmm, hadn't considered that. Editing now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: knowing which agents to under no circumstances send to recontain this thing when the site blows up and the site director is dead is pretty fucking important, and should absolutely not be restricted information. that is the most important thing about containment procedures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But the director is the only one who would approve site transfers, so limiting it to his knowledge would still screen people effectively. I dunno, I wrote that a few iterations ago, so it may not be the strongest idea. I'll think about it more.

EDIT: Okay, I'll just ask you guys. I personally think it's better with the first collapsible, but have no legitimate, in-universe reason for it. It could or couldn't be classsified and still be pretty much equivalent. Thoughts? Feelings? Haikus?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah. That should be widespread knowledge for anyone who would have any reason to come within a mile of this thing. I imagine pretty much everyone who works for the Foundation has had a thorough medical exam and likely knows their own blood type.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also, I don't see why the "attacks people with B/AB and gives them cardboard rabies" would be something requiring Level 4 clearance. And besides not seeming like a thing that would need to be kept that secret, it also means that the "additional containment procedures" have to be enforced solely by the site director, who almost certainly has more pressing things to do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm sorry. I still don't buy all the Serpent's Hand stuff. It's just a dog. After it bit someone and converted them any organization with any sense at all would've crated it and not let anyone ever get within biting range. So what if it went into a rage state as long as it was safely locked in a crate? They lost 24 people to it? I don't buy it. That's a level of incompetence I cannot believe. Someone sacrificing themselves to save their lover? Why? How is that the only option when it's no stronger than any normal dog?

This isn't a dog to the Foundation, it's an SCP, an object, and one that they don't want getting out. I see no reason to keep it in an above-ground courtyard versus a indoor containment cell where it can be safely prevented from being able to bit anyone.

You have a really interesting concept, and you paint a great visual of how it moves and behaves. I don't know why you needed to attach a weird side story about the Serpent's Hand. It's illogical and distracting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A cardboard dog that poops photos of poop. What.

SCP-1783 will play normally with humans in its presence, retrieving thrown toys and bones and returning them to the thrower.

I think the document would either indicate how it picks up objects, even if it's just "clings to the surface of the dog's head through an unknown means".

and promptly committed suicide via cyanide ingestion.

So the Serpent's Hand is so against the destruction of SCPs that its members would rather commit suicide than destroy an animate cardboard dog? Or is the thing indestructible? And even if it isn't indestructible, they should have been able to lock it up in a room and just leave it there without feeding it. Unless it can teleport through walls.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd think they'd put it in a courtyard just because it's a dog (I mean, they wouldn't build a courtyard for it, but they'd use one lying around) and there's not a particular risk of breach that they know of from keeping it there. Knowing how to avoid an incident from the beginning, the Foundation isn't particularly at risk at all. The terror the Serpent's Hand has for it comes from the bad experiences they had with it before they knew to keep B-positive individuals away from it. If the Foundation has another incident like 1783-1, it'd probably get crated, but given the fairly remote risk of an incident like that, there's no reason not to let it stay where it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is there a reason it can only be kept in a courtyard and not a containment cell? It doesn't seem to have any abilities that would prevent a much more secure containment. If it only excretes polaroids the Foundation could easily rig up an automated system to remove small pieces of paper from the floor of a room.

And now if it so much as sniffs out an employee with B antigens the S.O.P. is to sacrifice the poor sap to be turned into cardboard? Why? Because it won't calm down otherwise? Who cares? Aside from its weird appearance and virulent bite this is just a dog. Crate it and the problem is solved. I'm baffled that it's being treated like some uncontrollable Keter skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had forgotten about that one, to be honest. And now that I've reread it, I now dislike both the SCP and the supplement for unrelated (you know, quality-related) reasons.

However, I remember thinking that the Hand wasn't destroying the SCP, just destroying the Foundation's copy of it. More out of a tone of "silly kids, you're gonna shoot your eye out" than "BURN THE SKIP". Either way, good call.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't see the Serpent's Hand destroying an SCP.

Despite the fact that one of the (very) few pieces that mentions them has them doing that exact thing? http://www.scp-wiki.net/incident-report-x23
